title: Calcium Imaging of Cultured Cells with Fluo-4 Using Nikon Automated Microscopy
author: Akihiro Suto

steps.title: Place chamber slide on microscope stage,...
steps.instruction: Place chamber slide on microscope stage, select an objective for imaging at the required magnification. Typically a 20x Air objective will give a sweet spot for magnification and brightness. Immersion media objectives are desirable for greater than 20x magnification, however an objective heater may be necessary to maintain temperature of cells.

steps.title: Preheat the stage/cage incubator to 37°C...
steps.instruction: Preheat the stage/cage incubator to 37°C 1 - 2 hours ahead of imaging

steps.title: Flush the incubation chamber with CO...
steps.instruction: Flush the incubation chamber with CO 2 10-20 minutes ahead of imaging. Ensure the correct holder is used for the incubation chamber, and that there are no large openings for CO 2 to escape from the chamber

steps.title: Set acquisition parameters for excitation light...
steps.instruction: Set acquisition parameters for excitation light intensity Recommended at least 100FPS for Calcium imaging in cardiomyocytes, an exposure of 10ms or less is required.

steps.title: Crop the camera sensor to a...
steps.instruction: Crop the camera sensor to a smaller region of interest (ROI) to achieve higher framerates. Cropping height with increase framerate, cropping in width will not increase framerate. The smaller the ROI, the faster the framerate (FPS seen during the live-view in the bottom left corner) Acquisition menu

steps.title: Navigate to the centre of your...
steps.instruction: Navigate to the centre of your well and focus on your sample

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Search for the perfect focus system (PFS) offset and activate PFS This will stabilise the microscope against thermal fluctuations, and maintain focus across the sample.

steps.title: Import JOB definition - as below Multipoint Calcium Imaging
steps.instruction: Import JOB definition - as below Multipoint Calcium Imaging.bin Nikon Automated Microscopy: Import a JOB .bin file into JOBs Explorer by Mark Rigby

steps.title: Set which channel you want to image in E
steps.instruction: Set which channel you want to image in E.g. For Fluo-4, you would choose the Green channel on your Nikon microscope

steps.title: Set the number of timelapse recordings...
steps.instruction: Set the number of timelapse recordings to capture according to experimental requirements / size of well

steps.title: Set the duration of each individual recording
steps.instruction: Set the duration of each individual recording

steps.title: Ensure the sample is still in...
steps.instruction: Ensure the sample is still in focus, then press done to start acquisition.
